reviews.review_msg: Prof Anosh Irani at UBC, is very educated and passionate. He is inspirational and offers a lot of wisdom in his lectures. His lectures don't consist of lecture slides, instead he freely talks and gives examples. He gives a lot of examples for us to learn from. Grading is tough by TAs. It is not an easy A elective in my opinion, but interesting.
pretty chill class, just 3 major assignments and in class writing exercises. hes passionate and encourages student participation sometimes altho alot of people are not rlly locked in lol
There was no final so that was great.

Anosh is incredible in what he does and it's very clear from his lectures. We dissected many pieces of media, exposing us to a diverse range of examples. While the classes were great, it's unfortunate that your marks rely on 3 big writing assignments that are graded harshly by the TAs. Don't take if you're just looking for a GPA booster.
His lectures are solid, and the way he teaches is good. Nothing about the course is bad, I quite enjoyed the analysis of the pieces in class. Grades depend a lot on the TA you get.

Loved professor Irani; his lectures were so interesting, engaging, and fun! He doesn't use slides and assigns in-class exercises so attendance is important, but the content used is great and he usually ends class early. Unfortunately, your grade is dependant on only 3 projects and few in-class exercises, and grading is really tough. (@UBC)

crwr200 was pretty easy, anosh is very kind and cares about his students. crwr isnt an interest of mine but i was able to spend most classes doing other work and then just tune in for the quick in class participation assingments on canvas. only a few projects that were pretty easy and quick to do

I took this class to fill in a gap in my schedule, big mistake. His lectures were really fun, and he genuinely seems interested in teaching, but he grades HORRIBLY!!!! This is supposed to be an intro level course, but he's grading like I'm in my fourth year. I enjoyed the material, but it's not possible to get anything above a 79.
He will only rate you based on rubric and personal preferences. No artistic attempt will have a good ending with him

If you skip class there's literally nothing for you to work off of. No textbooks, no canvas modules, maybe just knowlege that you have an assignment due, and a bit of info about it via email. Despite that Anosh is a pretty good teacher. He's really caring and will help you get the grade you want, he just expects you to put in the work!

Anosh is hands down one of the best professors I've ever had. He has such a deep knowledge of his craft, and delivers it in the most inspiring and accessible way. He balances intensity with the occasional bit of humour, and is careful to remind us after an intense lecture or feedback session that we're learning, and that all we can do is our best.
